document.masterDocTitle: A Review Of The Subfamily Rhyssinae (Hymenoptera, Ichneumonidae) From The Ukrainian Carpathians

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A Review Of The Subfamily Rhyssinae (Hymenoptera, Ichneumonidae) From The Ukrainian Carpathians

caption.paragraph:
Fig. 5. Genus
Rhyssa
:
1 — Rhyssa amoena
, head (frontal view), female;
2 — Rhyssa persuasoria
, mesosoma (lateral view), female.

Рис. 5. Род
Rhyssa
:
1 — Rhyssa amoena
, голова (вид спереди), самка;
2 — Rhyssa persuasoria
, грудь (вид сбоку), самка.

subSubSection.paragraph: Coloration. Female. Generally body colour as in previous species, but white marks of head and mesosoma more abundant: stripes on eye orbits, frons, temples, pronotum partly, tegula, subtegular ridge, mesopleuron partly, scutellum, postscutellum and spots on propodeum white. Clypeus varying from reddish-brown to black. Legs generally red, all coxae varying from red to brown, sometimes with yellow spots, hind tibiae and tarsi fuscous.
M a l e. Body colour as in female, but face entirely yellow. Fore and mid coxae, trochanters and trochantelli yellow with red or (and) black spots.

V a r i a t i o n. In only one male , the fore and mid coxae, trochanters and trochantelli were entirely yellow and in a few males the pedicel has a small yellow spot, but the combination of these two characters were never found in the same specimen.
D i s t r i b u t i o n. A cosmopolitan species.
